dashboard.label: About
dashboard.definition:
{"title":"About","description":"Setup, documentation, and source links","visualizations":{"about_md":{"type":"splunk.markdown","options":{"markdown":"# Oura Health for Splunk

An end-to-end pipeline and Dashboard Studio app for visualizing Oura Ring data — sleep, heart rate, activity, wellness, and ring/battery status.

## Quick start
1. **Create an Oura developer app** and mint an OAuth token at [developer.ouraring.com](https://developer.ouraring.com).
2. **Create the `oura` index** — Settings → Indexes → New Index (leave the index type as **Events**, the default).
3. **Enable HTTP Event Collector (HEC)** — Settings → Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector → Global Settings (enable it; note the port).
4. **Create a HEC token tied to the `oura` index** — Data Inputs → HTTP Event Collector → New Token; set its allowed/default index to `oura`.
5. **Run the ingest script** (`oura_to_hec_with_phi.py`) to pull the Oura API into HEC (OAuth2 + PKCE, incremental, deduped); schedule it via cron.
6. **Install the two companion viz apps** (below) so the hypnogram and charge-status ring render.

The full step-by-step guide — Oura API, HEC URL variations, cron, backfill, troubleshooting — is in **INSTALL.md** in the source repo.

## Source & documentation
- **App source, ingest script & INSTALL.md:** [github.com/narwhaldc/oura-health-splunk](https://github.com/narwhaldc/oura-health-splunk)
- **Full setup guide:** [INSTALL.md](https://github.com/narwhaldc/oura-health-splunk/blob/main/INSTALL.md)

## Companion custom visualizations (install alongside)
- **hypnogram_viz** — sleep & HR-zone lane timelines: [github.com/narwhaldc/hypnogram_viz](https://github.com/narwhaldc/hypnogram_viz)
- **charge_ring_viz** — the ring charge-status gauge: [github.com/narwhaldc/charge_ring_viz](https://github.com/narwhaldc/charge_ring_viz)

## Scope
Single-user by design: it assumes one Oura account owner who is also the only person with access to the `oura` index — and therefore its personal health information (PHI). Multi-user access isolation and role-based access control (RBAC) are planned future work.

## License
Apache-2.0 · Copyright 2026 Tony Vincent","backgroundColor":"rgba(15,17,23,0.50)"}}},"dataSources":{},"inputs":{},"layout":{"type":"absolute","options":{"width":1440,"height":900,"display":"auto","backgroundImage":{"x":0,"y":0,"src":"/static/app/oura_health/bg_health.svg","sizeType":"cover"}},"structure":[{"item":"about_md","type":"block","position":{"x":20,"y":20,"w":1400,"h":860}}],"globalInputs":[]}}
